steps.name: create_pr
steps.summary: Create a pull request from workflow context data.

steps.name: get_pull_request
steps.summary: Fetch a pull request and expose it to downstream steps.

steps.name: merge_pull_request
steps.summary: Merge a pull request with workflow-selected merge options.

steps.name: verify_pull_request_state
steps.summary: Validate PR state before continuing a workflow.

steps.name: ai_suggest_pr_description
steps.summary: Generate PR title and body from branch changes using AI.

steps.name: prompt_for_pr_title
steps.summary: Prompt for a pull request title.

steps.name: prompt_for_pr_body
steps.summary: Prompt for a pull request body.

steps.name: prompt_for_issue_body_step
steps.summary: Prompt for free-form issue input before AI expansion.

steps.name: prompt_for_self_assign
steps.summary: Ask whether the current user should be assigned.

steps.name: prompt_for_labels
steps.summary: Prompt for GitHub labels to attach to an issue or PR.

steps.name: select_cli
steps.summary: Select which external CLI should be used by a workflow.

steps.name: ai_suggest_issue_title_and_body
steps.summary: Generate issue content from user input and templates using AI.

steps.name: preview_and_confirm_issue
steps.summary: Preview generated issue content and confirm creation.

steps.name: create_issue
steps.summary: Create the GitHub issue from workflow context data.

steps.name: select_pr_for_review
steps.summary: Select a pull request that needs response or review.

steps.name: fetch_pending_comments
steps.summary: Load unresolved PR comments into workflow context.

steps.name: check_clean_state
steps.summary: Ensure the current branch state is safe before checkout.

steps.name: checkout_pr_branch
steps.summary: Switch to the PR branch while preserving original branch context.

steps.name: review_comments
steps.summary: Guide the user through comment resolution and local changes.

steps.name: push_commits
steps.summary: Push local follow-up commits back to the PR branch.

steps.name: send_comment_replies
steps.summary: Submit replies for the reviewed comments.

steps.name: request_review
steps.summary: Request another review after applying comment changes.

steps.name: checkout_original_branch
steps.summary: Restore the original branch after review work completes.

steps.name: select_pr_for_code_review
steps.summary: Choose a PR to analyze in the advanced code review pipeline.

steps.name: fetch_pr_review_bundle
steps.summary: Collect the review bundle with PR, diff, files, and discussion context.

steps.name: build_change_manifest
steps.summary: Build a structured manifest of changed files and review targets.

steps.name: build_existing_comments_index
steps.summary: Index existing review comments to avoid duplicates.

steps.name: build_review_checklist
steps.summary: Prepare a review checklist from PR context.

steps.name: ai_review_plan
steps.summary: Ask AI to plan the directed review strategy.

steps.name: validate_review_plan
steps.summary: Validate the AI review plan before execution.

steps.name: resolve_review_context
steps.summary: Expand the precise review contexts needed for targeted analysis.

steps.name: ai_review_findings
steps.summary: Run targeted AI analysis and produce candidate findings.

steps.name: normalize_findings
steps.summary: Normalize raw AI findings into workflow-friendly structures.

steps.name: dedupe_findings
steps.summary: Remove duplicate or overlapping findings before submission.

steps.name: build_new_comment_actions
steps.summary: Translate validated findings into GitHub review actions.

steps.name: validate_review_actions
steps.summary: Validate review actions before posting comments.

steps.name: submit_review_actions
steps.summary: Submit review comments or review actions to GitHub.

steps.name: build_thread_review_candidates
steps.summary: Collect review threads that may need follow-up decisions.

steps.name: build_thread_review_contexts
steps.summary: Expand the context required to evaluate thread resolution.

steps.name: ai_thread_resolution
steps.summary: Ask AI whether open review threads should be resolved or reopened.

steps.name: normalize_thread_decisions
steps.summary: Normalize thread resolution decisions for workflow execution.

steps.name: build_thread_actions
steps.summary: Build GitHub actions for thread resolution decisions.

steps.name: create_worktree
steps.summary: Create an isolated worktree for GitHub-focused workflow tasks.

steps.name: cleanup_worktree
steps.summary: Remove a worktree created during workflow execution.

steps.name: ai_review_findings
steps.summary: Second AI call: find actionable problems in the exact code context.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): raw_findings (list | str): Raw AI output before normalization

steps.name: ai_review_plan
steps.summary: First AI call: decide which files to read and which checklist items apply.

steps.name: ai_suggest_issue_title_and_body
steps.summary: Use AI to suggest a title and description for a GitHub issue.

Inputs (from ctx.data): issue_body (str): Raw issue request entered by the user.
Outputs (saved to ctx.data): labels (list[str]): Suggested labels for the issue.
Returns: Success: If issue content is generated and accepted.
Skip: If AI is unavailable or the user rejects the generated issue.
Error: If required context is missing or generation fails.

steps.name: ai_suggest_pr_description
steps.summary: Generate PR title and description using PRAgent.

Inputs (from ctx.data): pr_head_branch (str): The head branch for the PR
Outputs (saved to ctx.data): ai_generated (bool): True if AI generated the content
Returns: Skip: AI not configured or user declined
Error: Failed to generate PR description

steps.name: ai_thread_resolution
steps.summary: AI call: decide what to do with each open thread.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): raw_thread_decisions (list): Raw AI output before normalization

steps.name: build_change_manifest
steps.summary: Build a structured manifest of the PR changes (no AI involved).

steps.name: build_existing_comments_index
steps.summary: Build a compact index of existing PR comments for deduplication.

steps.name: build_new_comment_actions
steps.summary: Convert deduplicated findings into ReviewActionProposal objects.

steps.name: build_review_checklist
steps.summary: Assemble the review checklist for this PR.

steps.name: build_thread_actions
steps.summary: Transform ThreadDecision objects into ReviewActionProposal objects.

Returns: Success, Skip (no actionable decisions), or Error

steps.name: build_thread_review_candidates
steps.summary: Select open inline threads worth AI analysis.

steps.name: build_thread_review_contexts
steps.summary: Enrich thread candidates with diff hunk context and full reply history.

steps.name: check_clean_state
steps.summary: Check that the working tree is clean before checking out the PR branch.

Returns: Exit: Uncommitted changes detected — user must stash manually

steps.name: checkout_original_branch
steps.summary: Restore the original branch after PR review.

Returns: Exit: No original branch saved or checkout failed

steps.name: checkout_pr_branch
steps.summary: Save the current branch and checkout the PR branch.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): original_branch (str): Branch to restore after review
Returns: Error: Failed to fetch or checkout

steps.name: cleanup_worktree
steps.summary: Cleanup a worktree created for PR review.

steps.name: create_issue
steps.summary: Create a new GitHub issue.

Inputs (from ctx.data): issue_title (str): Issue title.
issue_body (str): Issue body.
assignees (list, optional): Usernames to assign.
labels (list, optional): Labels to apply.
Returns: Success: If the issue is created successfully.
Error: If required context is missing or the GitHub call fails.

steps.name: create_pr
steps.summary: Creates a GitHub pull request using data from the workflow context.

Inputs (from ctx.data): pr_title (str): The title of the pull request.
pr_body (str, optional): The body/description of the pull request.
pr_head_branch (str): The branch with the new changes.
pr_is_draft (bool, optional): Whether to create the PR as a draft. Defaults to False.
pr_reviewers (list, optional): List of GitHub usernames or team slugs to request review from.
pr_excluded_reviewers (list, optional): List of GitHub usernames to exclude from team expansion.
pr_labels (list, optional): List of label names to add to the PR.
Configuration (from ctx.github.config):
auto_assign_prs (bool): If True, automatically assigns the PR to the current GitHub user.
Outputs (saved to ctx.data): pr_number (int): The number of the created pull request.
pr_url (str): The URL of the created pull request.
Returns: Success: If the PR is created successfully.
Error: If any required context arguments are missing or if the API call fails.

steps.name: create_worktree
steps.summary: Create a worktree for PR review.

steps.name: dedupe_findings
steps.summary: Remove findings that duplicate existing PR comments.

steps.name: fetch_pending_comments
steps.summary: Fetch unresolved review threads for the selected PR using GraphQL.

steps.name: fetch_pr_review_bundle
steps.summary: Fetch all data needed for a full PR review cycle.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): review_changed_files (List[str]): Changed file paths (may be subset for large PRs)
review_changed_files_with_stats (List[UIFileChange]): All files with add/del stats
pr_template (str | None): PR template content if available

steps.name: get_pull_request
steps.summary: Fetch a pull request and store it in workflow context.

Inputs (from ctx.data): pr_number (int): Pull request number to fetch.
Outputs (saved to ctx.data): pr_info: The fetched pull request object.
Returns: Success: If the pull request is fetched successfully.
Error: If required context is missing or the GitHub call fails.

steps.name: merge_pull_request
steps.summary: Merge a pull request using the configured GitHub client.

Inputs (from ctx.data): pr_number (int): Pull request number to merge.
merge_method (str, optional): Merge strategy.
commit_title (str, optional): Override commit title.
commit_message (str, optional): Override commit message.
Outputs (saved to ctx.data): merge_result: The GitHub merge result object.
Returns: Success: If the pull request is merged successfully.
Error: If required context is missing or the GitHub call fails.

steps.name: normalize_findings
steps.summary: Parse and validate raw AI output into Finding models.

steps.name: normalize_thread_decisions
steps.summary: Parse and validate raw AI output into ThreadDecision models.

steps.name: preview_and_confirm_issue
steps.summary: Show a preview of the AI-generated issue and ask for confirmation.

Inputs (from ctx.data): issue_title (str): Generated issue title.
issue_body (str): Generated issue body.
Returns: Success: If the user confirms the issue preview.
Error: If required context is missing, the user rejects, or the prompt is cancelled.

steps.name: prompt_for_issue_body_step
steps.summary: Interactively prompts the user for a GitHub issue body.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): issue_body (str): The body/description entered by the user.
Returns: Success: If the body was captured successfully.

steps.name: prompt_for_labels
steps.summary: Prompts the user to select labels for the issue.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): labels (list[str]): Labels selected by the user.
Returns: Success: If label selection completes successfully.
Skip: If the repository has no labels.
Error: If the GitHub client is unavailable or the prompt fails.

steps.name: prompt_for_pr_body
steps.summary: Interactively prompts the user for a Pull Request body.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): pr_body (str): The body/description entered by the user.
Returns: Success: If the body was captured successfully.

steps.name: prompt_for_pr_title
steps.summary: Interactively prompts the user for a Pull Request title.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): pr_title (str): The title entered by the user.
Returns: Success: If the title was captured successfully.
Error: If the user cancels or the title is empty.

steps.name: prompt_for_self_assign
steps.summary: Asks the user if they want to assign the issue to themselves.

Outputs (saved to ctx.data): assignees (list[str]): Updated assignees list when the user chooses self-assignment.
Returns: Success: If the assignment preference is captured successfully.
Error: If the GitHub client is unavailable or the prompt fails.

steps.name: push_commits
steps.summary: Push commits to PR branch.

Returns: Skip: No commits to push or user cancelled

steps.name: request_review
steps.summary: Re-request review from existing reviewers.

Returns: Skip: Push didn't succeed or user cancelled

steps.name: resolve_review_context
steps.summary: Fetch the exact code context according to the validated review plan.

steps.name: review_comments
steps.summary: Review all unresolved comment threads one by one and take action.

steps.name: select_cli
steps.summary: Ask user to explicitly choose which AI CLI to use for PR analysis.

Returns: Success with the chosen CLI name stored in ctx.data

steps.name: select_pr_for_code_review
steps.summary: List all open PRs and ask user to select one.

Returns: Success, Exit (no PRs or cancelled), or Error

steps.name: select_pr_for_review
steps.summary: Select a PR from the user's open PRs to review comments.

Returns: Exit: No PRs available or user cancelled

steps.name: send_comment_replies
steps.summary: Send comment replies (both text responses and commit hashes).

Returns: Skip: No replies to send or user cancelled

steps.name: submit_review_actions
steps.summary: Submit approved ReviewActionProposal objects to GitHub.

Returns: Success, Skip (no approved actions), or Error

steps.name: validate_review_actions
steps.summary: Present each ReviewActionProposal to the user for approval, editing, or skipping.

steps.name: validate_review_plan
steps.summary: Validate the AI-generated ReviewPlan against local semantic rules.

Returns: Success or Error (halts workflow on validation failure)

steps.name: verify_pull_request_state
steps.summary: Verify a pull request is currently in the expected state.

Inputs (from ctx.data): pr_number (int): Pull request number to inspect.
expected_state (str): Expected pull request state.
Outputs (saved to ctx.data): verified_pr_info: The pull request object when verification succeeds.
Returns: Success: If the pull request is in the expected state.
Error: If required context is missing, verification fails, or the GitHub call fails.
